items.headline: Hezbollah chief: 'Lebanese resistance will not surrender, the battlefield will speak'
items.summary: Hezbollah Secretary General Sheikh Naim Qassem has said that the Lebanese Islamic resistance will confront the ongoing Israeli-American aggression against the people of Lebanon and demanded immediate implementation of the November 2024 ceasefire agreement.

items.headline: Fidan: 'Israel cannot live without an enemy' and seeks to declare Turkey as one
items.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan says Israel "cannot live without an enemy" and the incumbent cabinet led by Benjamin Netanyahu is now seeking to declare the Ankara government as its new enemy.

items.headline: Yemen vows military escalation if US, Israel resume war on Iran
items.summary: Yemen's Foreign Ministry says the Arab country's position remains steadfast in active participation and escalation of military operations if the United States and the Israeli regime resume their war of aggression against Iran and the Axis of Resistance.

items.headline: Iran resumes rail services on routes damaged by war
items.summary:
Authorities have in recent days begun reconstruction work on bridges, railway lines and other infrastructure hit during nearly 40 days of fighting.
The post Iran resumes rail services on routes damaged by war appeared first on Vanguard News.

items.headline: Iran threatens ports in West Asia as US military set to impose shipping blockade
items.summary: US Central Command announced the blockade would begin on Monday at 10 am EDT or 5:30 pm in Iran, and would be ‘enforced impartially against vessels of all nations entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas’

items.headline: Trump Is About to Make the Oil Crisis Even Worse
items.summary: Iran and oil expert Gregory Brew discusses President Trump’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and what a best-case scenario for the oil crisis looks like now.

items.headline: Trump’s threat to blockade Hormuz: Why it’s war’s latest major escalation
items.summary: Analysts warn Washington's blockade could harm the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran.

items.headline: UK Rejects US Iran Blockade as Macron Pushes Multinational Talks to Reopen Vital Strait of Hormuz

items.headline: U.S. Blockade Draws New Red Lines in Strait of Hormuz Crisis

items.headline: What To Know About The US Naval Blockade Of Iran
items.summary: A US naval blockade on ships entering Iranian ports and coastal areas has come into effect, following failed peace talks between US and Iranian negotiators over the weekend.

items.headline: JD Vance Proves Irony Is Dead as He Calls Out “Preposterous” Behavior

items.headline: In 2011, Barack Obama said it was time to ‘pivot’ to Asia. But 15 years later, the U.S. is still at war in the Middle East
items.summary: “Rebalancing to Asia is highly relevant to America’s national interests, but it has been undercut by many bad decisions.”

items.headline: Iran war diverts US military and attention from Asia ahead of Trump's summit with China's leader
items.summary: President Barack Obama in 2011 declared it was time to leave behind the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan and “pivot” to Asia to counter China's rise

items.headline: Iran war diverts US military, attention from Asia ahead of Trump's summit with China's leader

items.headline: Goldman Sachs warning on consumers: Gas price spike will be felt
items.summary: Consumers can't outrun higher gas prices forever.

items.headline: War in Middle East hampering Finland's economic recovery, OP forecast says
items.summary: OP Pohjola anticipates a slow but gradual recovery for the Finnish economy, although the war could hinder progress.

items.headline: Markets’ dilemma: Trust the bark or wag of oil prices
items.summary: Split signals. Surge in physical oil prices alongside a steep fall in futures underscores a stark divergence, with the gap between the two widening to historic highs

items.headline: How Pakistan managed to get the US and Iran to a ceasefire
items.summary: Backchannel talks led by Pakistan result in temporary ceasefire after weeks of fighting.

items.headline: Saudi Pipeline Attack Removes 700,000 Barrels Daily from Oil Exports
items.summary: The Saudi energy ministry confirmed on Thursday that Iranian strikes on the kingdom's East-West pipeline have reduced its export capacity by approximately 700,000 barrels per day (bpd). The ministry stated the attacks hit a pumping station on the vital pipeli…

items.headline: Russia Expands Gasoline Export Ban, Faces Major Oil Outages Due to Drone Strikes
items.summary: The Russian government has expanded a ban on gasoline exports to include producers of the fuel, according to a report from Interfax news agency [1]. The ban, which now includes all gasoline producers, is scheduled to remain in effect until July 31. It expands…
